questions.q: Before upgrading the Kubernetes version on a worker node with `kubeadm upgrade node`, what action MUST be performed on the control plane?
questions.explanation: Before upgrading a worker node, you must safely evict all running workloads by 'draining' the node. `--ignore-daemonsets` is usually required because DaemonSet pods cannot be evicted by the standard eviction API.

questions.q: You try to use `kubectl edit pod` to change the `env` variables of a running Pod, but the API server rejects the change. Why?
options: You do not have cluster-admin privileges.
The Pod is running in the kube-system namespace.
Only a few fields (like image, tolerations, activeDeadlineSeconds) can be edited on a live Pod. Other changes require recreating the Pod.
You must drain the node before editing the Pod.
questions.explanation: Pods are mostly immutable once created. If you need to change fields like environment variables, volumes, or commands, you must extract the YAML (`kubectl get pod -o yaml`), edit it, delete the old pod, and apply the new YAML.
